title.value: Cross-version Profile for R4.ExampleScenario for use in FHIR R5

description.value: This cross-version profile allows R4 ExampleScenario content to be represented via FHIR R5 ExampleScenario resources.

definition.value: Example of workflow instance.

human.value: Name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation
Must have actors if status is active or required
Must have processes if status is active or required
Actor keys must be unique
Actor titles must be unique
Instance keys must be unique
Instance titles must be unique
Process titles must be unique

definition.value: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

comment.value: Within the context of the FHIR RESTful interactions, the resource has an id except for cases like the create and conditional update. Otherwise, the use of the resouce id depends on the given use case.

definition.value: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: A set of rules under which this content was created

definition.value: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

comment.value: Asserting this rule set restricts the content to be only understood by a limited set of trading partners. This inherently limits the usefulness of the data in the long term. However, the existing health eco-system is highly fractured, and not yet ready to define, collect, and exchange data in a generally computable sense. Wherever possible, implementers and/or specification writers should avoid using this element. Often, when used, the URL is a reference to an implementation guide that defines these special rules as part of its narrative along with other profiles, value sets, etc.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: This element is labeled as a modifier because the implicit rules may provide additional knowledge about the resource that modifies its meaning or interpretation

definition.value: The base language in which the resource is written.

comment.value: Language is provided to support indexing and accessibility (typically, services such as text to speech use the language tag). The html language tag in the narrative applies to the narrative. The language tag on the resource may be used to specify the language of other presentations generated from the data in the resource. Not all the content has to be in the base language. The Resource.language should not be assumed to apply to the narrative automatically. If a language is specified, it should it also be specified on the div element in the html (see rules in HTML5 for information about the relationship between xml:lang and the html lang attribute).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

description.value: IETF language tag for a human language

short.value: Text summary of the resource, for human interpretation

definition.value: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

comment.value: Contained resources do not have a narrative. Resources that are not contained SHOULD have a narrative. In some cases, a resource may only have text with little or no additional discrete data (as long as all minOccurs=1 elements are satisfied). This may be necessary for data from legacy systems where information is captured as a "text blob" or where text is additionally entered raw or narrated and encoded information is added later.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, nor can they have their own independent transaction scope. This is allowed to be a Parameters resource if and only if it is referenced by a resource that provides context/meaning.

comment.value: This should never be done when the content can be identified properly, as once identification is lost, it is extremely difficult (and context dependent) to restore it again. Contained resources may have profiles and tags in their meta elements, but SHALL NOT have security labels.

definition.value: An Extension

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

definition.value: R4: `ExampleScenario.workflow` (new:canonical(ExampleScenario))

comment.value: Element `ExampleScenario.workflow` has a context of ExampleScenario based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ExampleScenario`. Element `ExampleScenario.workflow` has no mapping targets in FHIR R5. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the resource that contains them

short.value: Canonical identifier for this example scenario, represented as a URI (globally unique)

definition.value: An absolute URI that is used to identify this example scenario when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which an authoritative instance of this example scenario is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the example scenario is stored on different servers.

comment.value:
Can be a urn:uuid: or a urn:oid: but real http: addresses are preferred. Multiple instances may share the same URL if they have a distinct version.
The determination of when to create a new version of a resource (same url, new version) vs. defining a new artifact is up to the author. Considerations for making this decision are found in [Technical and Business Versions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/resource.html#versions).
In some cases, the resource can no longer be found at the stated url, but the url itself cannot change. Implementations can use the [meta.source](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/resource.html#meta) element to indicate where the current master source of the resource can be found.

requirements.value: Allows the example scenario to be referenced by a single globally unique identifier.

human.value: URL should not contain | or # - these characters make processing canonical references problematic
All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Additional identifier for the example scenario

definition.value: A formal identifier that is used to identify this example scenario when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

comment.value: Typically, this is used for identifiers that can go in an HL7 V3 II (instance identifier) data type, and can then identify this example scenario outside of FHIR, where it is not possible to use the logical URI.

requirements.value: Allows externally provided and/or usable business identifiers to be easily associated with the module.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Business version of the example scenario

definition.value: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the example scenario when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the example scenario author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

comment.value: There may be different example scenario instances that have the same identifier but different versions. The version can be appended to the url in a reference to allow a reference to a particular business version of the example scenario with the format [url]|[version]. The version SHOULD NOT contain a '#' - see [Business Version](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/resource.html#bv-format).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which is more current.

comment.value: If set as a string, this is a FHIRPath expression that has two additional context variables passed in - %version1 and %version2 and will return a negative number if version1 is newer, a positive number if version2 and a 0 if the version ordering can't be successfully be determined.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Temporarily retained for tooling purposes.

comment.value: Not needed as code will never be generated from one of these

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Name for this example scenario (human friendly)

definition.value: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the ExampleScenario.

comment.value: This name does not need to be machine-processing friendly and may contain punctuation, white-space, etc.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: The status of this example scenario. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

comment.value:
Allows filtering of example scenarios that are appropriate for use versus not.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/canonicalresource.html#localization).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: This is labeled as "Is Modifier" because applications should not use a retired {{title}} without due consideration

description.value: The lifecycle status of an artifact.

short.value: For testing purposes, not real usage

definition.value: A Boolean value to indicate that this example scenario is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

comment.value: Allows filtering of example scenarios that are appropriate for use versus not.

requirements.value: Enables experimental content to be developed following the same lifecycle that would be used for a production-level example scenario.

meaningWhenMissing.value: If absent, this resource is treated as though it is not experimental.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: The date (and optionally time) when the example scenario was last significantly changed. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the example scenario changes. (e.g. the 'content logical definition').

comment.value:
The date is often not tracked until the resource is published, but may be present on draft content. Note that this is not the same as the resource last-modified-date, since the resource may be a secondary representation of the example scenario. Additional specific dates may be added as extensions or be found by consulting Provenances associated with past versions of the resource.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/canonicalresource.html#localization).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Name of the publisher/steward (organization or individual)

definition.value: The name of the organization or individual responsible for the release and ongoing maintenance of the example scenario.

comment.value: Usually an organization but may be an individual. The publisher (or steward) of the example scenario is the organization or individual primarily responsible for the maintenance and upkeep of the example scenario. This is not necessarily the same individual or organization that developed and initially authored the content. The publisher is the primary point of contact for questions or issues with the example scenario. This item SHOULD be populated unless the information is available from context.

requirements.value: Helps establish the "authority/credibility" of the example scenario. May also allow for contact.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

comment.value:
May be a web site, an email address, a telephone number, etc.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/canonicalresource.html#localization).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Natural language description of the ExampleScenario

definition.value: A free text natural language description of the ExampleScenario from a consumer's perspective.

comment.value: This description can be used to capture details such as comments about misuse. It is not a rendering of the ExampleScenario as conveyed in the 'text' field of the resource itself.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: The context that the content is intended to support

definition.value: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate example scenario instances.

comment.value: When multiple useContexts are specified, there is no expectation that all or any of the contexts apply.

requirements.value: Assist in searching for appropriate content.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Intended jurisdiction for example scenario (if applicable)

definition.value: A legal or geographic region in which the example scenario is intended to be used.

comment.value:
It may be possible for the example scenario to be used in jurisdictions other than those for which it was originally designed or intended.
DEPRECATION NOTE: For consistency, implementations are encouraged to migrate to using the new 'jurisdiction' code in the useContext element. (I.e. useContext.code indicating http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/usage-context-type#jurisdiction and useContext.valueCodeableConcept indicating the jurisdiction.)

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

description.value: Countries and regions within which this artifact is targeted for use.

short.value: The purpose of the example, e.g. to illustrate a scenario

definition.value: What the example scenario resource is created for. This should not be used to show the business purpose of the scenario itself, but the purpose of documenting a scenario.

comment.value: This element does not describe the usage of the example scenario. Instead, it provides traceability of ''why'' the resource is either needed or ''why'' it is defined as it is. This may be used to point to source materials or specifications that drove the structure of this example scenario.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A copyright statement relating to the example scenario and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the example scenario.

requirements.value: Consumers must be able to determine any legal restrictions on the use of the example scenario and/or its content.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A short string (<50 characters), suitable for inclusion in a page footer that identifies the copyright holder, effective period, and optionally whether rights are resctricted. (e.g. 'All rights reserved', 'Some rights reserved').

comment.value: The (c) symbol should NOT be included in this string. It will be added by software when rendering the notation. Full details about licensing, restrictions, warrantees, etc. goes in the more general 'copyright' element.

requirements.value: Defines the content expected to be rendered in all representations of the artifact.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A system or person who shares or receives an instance within the scenario.

requirements.value: This is optional to allow for ExampleScenarios that are still draft

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Actor should be referenced in at least one operation
actor.key canot be 'OTHER'

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: ID or acronym of the actor

definition.value: A unique string within the scenario that is used to reference the actor.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: The category of actor - person or system.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

description.value: The type of actor - system or human.

definition.value: The human-readable name for the actor used when rendering the scenario.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: An explanation of who/what the actor is and its role in the scenario.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A single data collection that is shared as part of the scenario.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
StructureVersion is required if structureType is not FHIR (but may still be present even if FHIR)
instance.content is only allowed if there are no instance.versions
Version keys must be unique within an instance
Version titles must be unique within an instance
Instance should be referenced in at least one location
Instance version should be referenced in at least one operation

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: ID or acronym of the instance

definition.value: A unique string within the scenario that is used to reference the instance.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A code indicating the kind of data structure (FHIR resource or some other standard) this is an instance of.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

description.value: The structure that defines the instance

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

description.value: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

definition.value: An Extension

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: R4: The type of the resource

definition.value: R4: `ExampleScenario.instance.resourceType`

comment.value: Element `ExampleScenario.instance.resourceType` is mapped to FHIR R5 element `ExampleScenario.instance.structureType` as `RelatedTo`.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

definition.value: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

comment.value: The URI may be an OID (urn:oid:...) or a UUID (urn:uuid:...). OIDs and UUIDs SHALL be references to the HL7 OID registry. Otherwise, the URI should come from HL7's list of FHIR defined special URIs or it should be an absolute reference to some definition that establishes the system clearly and unambiguously.

requirements.value: Need to be unambiguous about the source of the definition of the symbol.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Version of the system - if relevant

definition.value: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

comment.value: Where the terminology does not clearly define what string should be used to identify code system versions, the recommendation is to use the date (expressed in FHIR date format) on which that version was officially published as the version date.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Symbol in syntax defined by the system

definition.value: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

requirements.value: Need to refer to a particular code in the system.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

requirements.value: Need to be able to carry a human-readable meaning of the code for readers that do not know the system.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: C*E.2 - but note this is not well followed

short.value: If this coding was chosen directly by the user

definition.value: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

comment.value: Amongst a set of alternatives, a directly chosen code is the most appropriate starting point for new translations. There is some ambiguity about what exactly 'directly chosen' implies, and trading partner agreement may be needed to clarify the use of this element and its consequences more completely.

requirements.value: This has been identified as a clinical safety criterium - that this exact system/code pair was chosen explicitly, rather than inferred by the system based on some rules or language processing.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: fhir:Coding.userSelected fhir:mapsTo dt:CDCoding.codingRationale. fhir:Coding.userSelected fhir:hasMap fhir:Coding.userSelected.map. fhir:Coding.userSelected.map a fhir:Map; fhir:target dt:CDCoding.codingRationale. fhir:Coding.userSelected\#true a [ fhir:source "true"; fhir:target dt:CDCoding.codingRationale\#O ]

definition.value: Conveys the version of the data structure instantiated. I.e. what release of FHIR, X12, OpenEHR, etc. is instance compliant with.

meaningWhenMissing.value: If the structureType is a FHIR resource, then the version is the same FHIR version this ExampleScenario is expressed in.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Refers to a profile, template or other ruleset the instance adheres to.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A short descriptive label the instance to be used in tables or diagrams.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: An explanation of what the instance contains and what it's for.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Points to an instance (typically an example) that shows the data that would corespond to this instance.

comment.value: If not conveying FHIR data or not using the same version of FHIR as this ExampleScenario instance, the reference must be to a Binary.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Represents the instance as it was at a specific time-point.

comment.value: Not used if an instance doesn't change

requirements.value: Allows representing the 'same' instance as it evolves throughout the scenario.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: ID or acronym of the version

definition.value: A unique string within the instance that is used to reference the version of the instance.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A short descriptive label the version to be used in tables or diagrams.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: An explanation of what this specific version of the instance contains and represents.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Points to an instance (typically an example) that shows the data that would flow at this point in the scenario.

comment.value: If not conveying FHIR data or not using the same version of FHIR as this ExampleScenario instance, the reference must be to a Binary.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: References to other instances that can be found within this instance (e.g. the observations contained in a bundle).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
InstanceReference must be a key of an instance defined in the ExampleScenario
versionReference must be specified if the referenced instance defines versions
versionReference must be a key of a version within the instance pointed to by instanceReference

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

definition.value: A reference to the key of an instance found within this one.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A reference to the key of a specific version of an instance in this instance.

comment.value: Required if the referenced instance has versions

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A group of operations that represents a significant step within a scenario.

comment.value: Some scenarios might describe only one process.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Processes must have steps if ExampleScenario status is active or required

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

definition.value: A short descriptive label the process to be used in tables or diagrams.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: An explanation of what the process represents and what it does.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Description of the initial state of the actors, environment and data before the process starts.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Description of the final state of the actors, environment and data after the process has been successfully completed.

comment.value: Alternate steps might not result in all post conditions holding

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A significant action that occurs as part of the process.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Alternative titles must be unique within a step
Can have a process, a workflow, one or more operations or none of these, but cannot have a combination

expression.value: (process.exists() implies workflow.empty() and operation.empty()) and (workflow.exists() implies operation.empty())

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

definition.value: The sequential number of the step, e.g. 1.2.5.

comment.value: If step numbers are simultaneous, they will be the same. Numbers don't have to be numeric. E.g. '2c)' is a valid step number

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Indicates that the step is a complex sub-process with its own steps.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.
An Extension

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

definition.value: R4: `ExampleScenario.process.step.process` additional types (ExampleScenario.process)

comment.value: Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.process` is mapped to FHIR R5 element `ExampleScenario.process.step.process` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`. The mappings for `ExampleScenario.process.step.process` do not cover the following types: ExampleScenario.process.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

definition.value: The sequential number of the step, e.g. 1.2.5.

comment.value: If step numbers are simultaneous, they will be the same. Numbers don't have to be numeric. E.g. '2c)' is a valid step number

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Indicates that the step is a complex sub-process with its own steps.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Indicates that the step is defined by a seaparate scenario instance.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: The step represents a single operation invoked on receiver by sender.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
If specified, initiator must be a key of an actor within the ExampleScenario
If specified, receiver must be a key of an actor within the ExampleScenario

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

definition.value: The standardized type of action (FHIR or otherwise).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A short descriptive label the step to be used in tables or diagrams.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: The system that invokes the action/transmits the data.

comment.value: This must either be the 'key' of one of the actors defined in this scenario or the special keyword 'OTHER' if the initiator is not one of the actors defined for the scenario. (Multiple references to 'OTHER' don't necessarily indicate the same actor.)

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: The system on which the action is invoked/receives the data.

comment.value: This must either be the 'key' of one of the actors defined in this scenario or the special keyword 'OTHER' if the receiver is not one of the actors defined for the scenario. (Multiple references to 'OTHER' don't necessarily indicate the same actor.) In some cases, receiver could be same as sender if representing a local operation

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: An explanation of what the operation represents and what it does.

comment.value: This should contain information not already present in the process step. It's more of a description of what the operation in general does - if not already evident from the operation.type

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: If false, the initiator is deactivated right after the operation.

comment.value: De-activation of an actor means they have no further role until such time as they are the recipient of an operation.

requirements.value: The notion of 'activation' in a flow diagram is used to indicate 'who has responsibility/is still doing work'.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: If false, the receiver is deactivated right after the operation.

comment.value: De-activation of an actor means they have no further role until such time as they are the recipient of an operation.

requirements.value: The notion of 'activation' in a flow diagram is used to indicate 'who has responsibility/is still doing work'.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A reference to the instance that is transmitted from requester to receiver as part of the invocation of the operation.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A reference to the instance that is transmitted from receiver to requester as part of the operation's synchronous response (if any).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Indicates an alternative step that can be taken instead of the sub-process, scenario or operation. E.g. to represent non-happy-path/exceptional/atypical circumstances.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

definition.value: The label to display for the alternative that gives a sense of the circumstance in which the alternative should be invoked.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A human-readable description of the alternative explaining when the alternative should occur rather than the base step.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Indicates the operation, sub-process or scenario that happens if the alternative option is selected.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: If true, indicates that, following this step, there is a pause in the flow and the subsequent step will occur at some later time (triggered by some event).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Indicates that the step is defined by a seaparate scenario instance.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: The step represents a single operation invoked on receiver by sender.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
If specified, initiator must be a key of an actor within the ExampleScenario
If specified, receiver must be a key of an actor within the ExampleScenario

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.
An Extension

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: R4: The sequential number of the interaction (new)

definition.value: R4: `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.number` (new:string)

comment.value: Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.number` has a context of ExampleScenario.process.step.operation based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ExampleScenario`. Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.number` has no mapping targets in FHIR R5. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

definition.value: The standardized type of action (FHIR or otherwise).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

description.value: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

definition.value: An Extension

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: R4: The type of operation - CRUD additional types

definition.value: R4: `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.type` additional types (string) additional types from child elements (value)

comment.value: Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.type` is mapped to FHIR R5 element `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.type` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`. The mappings for `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.type` do not cover the following types: string. The mappings for `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.type` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: value.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

definition.value: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

comment.value: The URI may be an OID (urn:oid:...) or a UUID (urn:uuid:...). OIDs and UUIDs SHALL be references to the HL7 OID registry. Otherwise, the URI should come from HL7's list of FHIR defined special URIs or it should be an absolute reference to some definition that establishes the system clearly and unambiguously.

requirements.value: Need to be unambiguous about the source of the definition of the symbol.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Version of the system - if relevant

definition.value: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

comment.value: Where the terminology does not clearly define what string should be used to identify code system versions, the recommendation is to use the date (expressed in FHIR date format) on which that version was officially published as the version date.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Symbol in syntax defined by the system

definition.value: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

requirements.value: Need to refer to a particular code in the system.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

requirements.value: Need to be able to carry a human-readable meaning of the code for readers that do not know the system.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: C*E.2 - but note this is not well followed

short.value: If this coding was chosen directly by the user

definition.value: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

comment.value: Amongst a set of alternatives, a directly chosen code is the most appropriate starting point for new translations. There is some ambiguity about what exactly 'directly chosen' implies, and trading partner agreement may be needed to clarify the use of this element and its consequences more completely.

requirements.value: This has been identified as a clinical safety criterium - that this exact system/code pair was chosen explicitly, rather than inferred by the system based on some rules or language processing.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: fhir:Coding.userSelected fhir:mapsTo dt:CDCoding.codingRationale. fhir:Coding.userSelected fhir:hasMap fhir:Coding.userSelected.map. fhir:Coding.userSelected.map a fhir:Map; fhir:target dt:CDCoding.codingRationale. fhir:Coding.userSelected\#true a [ fhir:source "true"; fhir:target dt:CDCoding.codingRationale\#O ]

definition.value: A short descriptive label the step to be used in tables or diagrams.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: The system that invokes the action/transmits the data.

comment.value: This must either be the 'key' of one of the actors defined in this scenario or the special keyword 'OTHER' if the initiator is not one of the actors defined for the scenario. (Multiple references to 'OTHER' don't necessarily indicate the same actor.)

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: The system on which the action is invoked/receives the data.

comment.value: This must either be the 'key' of one of the actors defined in this scenario or the special keyword 'OTHER' if the receiver is not one of the actors defined for the scenario. (Multiple references to 'OTHER' don't necessarily indicate the same actor.) In some cases, receiver could be same as sender if representing a local operation

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: An explanation of what the operation represents and what it does.

comment.value: This should contain information not already present in the process step. It's more of a description of what the operation in general does - if not already evident from the operation.type

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: If false, the initiator is deactivated right after the operation.

comment.value: De-activation of an actor means they have no further role until such time as they are the recipient of an operation.

requirements.value: The notion of 'activation' in a flow diagram is used to indicate 'who has responsibility/is still doing work'.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: If false, the receiver is deactivated right after the operation.

comment.value: De-activation of an actor means they have no further role until such time as they are the recipient of an operation.

requirements.value: The notion of 'activation' in a flow diagram is used to indicate 'who has responsibility/is still doing work'.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A reference to the instance that is transmitted from requester to receiver as part of the invocation of the operation.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A reference to the instance that is transmitted from receiver to requester as part of the operation's synchronous response (if any).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Indicates an alternative step that can be taken instead of the sub-process, scenario or operation. E.g. to represent non-happy-path/exceptional/atypical circumstances.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

definition.value: The label to display for the alternative that gives a sense of the circumstance in which the alternative should be invoked.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: A human-readable description of the alternative explaining when the alternative should occur rather than the base step.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: Indicates the operation, sub-process or scenario that happens if the alternative option is selected.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: If true, indicates that, following this step, there is a pause in the flow and the subsequent step will occur at some later time (triggered by some event).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

definition.value: R4: `ExampleScenario.workflow` (new:canonical(ExampleScenario))

comment.value: Element `ExampleScenario.workflow` has a context of ExampleScenario based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ExampleScenario`. Element `ExampleScenario.workflow` has no mapping targets in FHIR R5. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

short.value: R4: The type of the resource

definition.value: R4: `ExampleScenario.instance.resourceType`

comment.value: Element `ExampleScenario.instance.resourceType` is mapped to FHIR R5 element `ExampleScenario.instance.structureType` as `RelatedTo`.

definition.value: R4: `ExampleScenario.process.step.process` additional types (ExampleScenario.process)

comment.value: Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.process` is mapped to FHIR R5 element `ExampleScenario.process.step.process` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`. The mappings for `ExampleScenario.process.step.process` do not cover the following types: ExampleScenario.process.

short.value: R4: The sequential number of the interaction (new)

definition.value: R4: `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.number` (new:string)

comment.value: Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.number` has a context of ExampleScenario.process.step.operation based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ExampleScenario`. Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.number` has no mapping targets in FHIR R5. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

short.value: R4: The type of operation - CRUD additional types

definition.value: R4: `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.type` additional types (string) additional types from child elements (value)

comment.value: Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.type` is mapped to FHIR R5 element `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.type` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`. The mappings for `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.type` do not cover the following types: string. The mappings for `ExampleScenario.process.step.operation.type` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: value.
